name: The Blue Carbuncle
description: A brilliant blue carbuncle (a large gemstone, likely a blue garnet or sapphire) of extraordinary value, described as having a history of blood and misfortune. The stone was stolen from the Countess of Morcar at the Hotel Cosmopolitan. It passes through a remarkable chain of hands: from the thief (John Horner, wrongly accused) to a goose seller, concealed inside a Christmas goose, and ultimately discovered by Holmes when the goose is cut open at 221B Baker Street.
significance: One of the most charming artifacts in the canon — a priceless gem discovered inside a Christmas goose. The case is notable for Holmes's decision to release the actual thief (James Ryder) rather than prosecute him, reasoning that imprisonment would make a worse man of him. The carbuncle itself is a MacGuffin that drives a comic chain-of-custody mystery.
